sections.paragraph:
ἐκλανθάνομαι pf. ἐκλέλησμαι (Hom. et al.; Philo, Leg. All. 3, 92) forget (altogether) τινός (Polyb. 5, 48, 6; POxy 1203, 8 [I a.d.]; Ps 12:2 Sym.; Philo, De Jos. 99; Jos., Ant. 4, 53; 7, 318) τῆς παρακλήσεως Hb 12:5.—M-M s.v. ἐκλανθάνω.

  1. pf. = perfect

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 305
